rules.title: Shai-Hulud: self-propagating npm supply chain attack via compromised maintainer tokens
rules.summary: First known self-propagating npm supply chain attack. Malicious postinstall scripts harvested npm tokens, environment variables, and cloud credentials using TruffleHog, then used stolen tokens to publish malicious versions of additional packages. Exfiltrated data was pushed to public GitHub repos named Shai-Hulud.

aliases.value: Wiz Research: Shai-Hulud npm supply chain attack analysis

host_indicators.notes: Shell script that creates shai-hulud branch and uploads malicious GitHub Actions workflow payload.
Shell script that migrates private organizational repos to public personal repos for exfiltration.
Temporary working directory for cloning operations during repo migration.
Webhook.site endpoint used for exfiltrating stolen secrets.

remediation.summary: Pin affected packages to known-good versions, regenerate lockfiles, rotate ALL npm tokens, GitHub tokens, cloud credentials, and CI/CD secrets that were present in the environment during npm install.
steps: Pin all affected packages to their last known-good version (the version before the ones listed in this rule).
Rotate ALL npm tokens immediately, the attack propagates via stolen npm tokens.
Rotate ALL GitHub tokens and SSH keys.
Rotate ALL cloud credentials (AWS, GCP, Azure) that were accessible via environment variables or IMDS.
Check GitHub for unauthorized branches named 'shai-hulud' in your repositories.
Check for unauthorized public repositories created under compromised GitHub accounts.
Review GitHub Actions workflow files for unauthorized modifications.
Re-run gouvernante to verify a clean scan (exit code 0).
Report status to your security team.
